_note: Live reference dossiers extracted from structure.json. Tradition architecture, hour maps, component inventories, horologion file details, COE calendar model. Updated in sync with structure.json.

east_syriac_tradition.status: OPERATIONAL — v2.8.1. All seven canonical hours implemented. Cathedral and monastic sequences for all hours. Full Qdham/Wathar Qanona rotation (layered hour-specific + seasonal for Sapra/Ramsha/Lelya), Marmitha psalmody, Barekmar intercessions (all 9 components now present), and Onitha-with-Qala rendering active in monastic mode.
east_syriac_tradition.entry_point: selectMode('east-syriac') — hides BCP and Ethiopian sidebars, shows east-syriac-settings, awaits hydrateForEastSyriac(), calls renderOffice()
east_syriac_tradition.component_count_note: 83 (v2.8.0) + 9 Barekmar components (v2.8.1) = 92. Duplicate removal of 3 components (esy-suraya, esy-karozutha, esy-concluding-blessing second copies) does not reduce logical count as duplicates carried no unique content.

sapra.title: Sapra — Morning Prayer
sapra.qanona_note: v2.8.1: esy-qanona-sapra now rendered first (hour-specific frame), followed by seasonal esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}

qutaa.title: Quta'a — Third Hour
qutaa.theological_note: Ps. 19 — sun going forth; Ps. 24 — King of glory entering; Ps. 25 — morning supplication. Hour of the Descent of the Holy Spirit.

endana.title: Endana — Sixth Hour
endana.theological_note: Ps. 119 (portion) — the longest psalm at the longest hour; Songs of Ascent for the midday pause.

dtsha-sain.title: D-tsha' Sa'in — Ninth Hour
dtsha-sain.theological_note: Hour of the Crucifixion. Ps. 116 — the cup of salvation as direct Passion type.

ramsha.title: Ramsha — Evening Prayer
ramsha.qanona_note: v2.8.1: esy-qanona-ramsha now rendered first (hour-specific frame), followed by seasonal esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}

lelya.title: Lelya — Night Office
lelya.qanona_note: v2.8.1: esy-qanona-lelya now rendered first (hour-specific frame), followed by seasonal esy-qanona-{season}-{cycle}

subaa.title: Suba'a — Compline
subaa.theological_note: Ps. 4 — ancient night psalm; Ps. 91 — the protection psalm; Ps. 134 — night doxology. Unique concluding elements: examination of conscience, Nunc Dimittis (Hullala d-Sham'un), and distinct Compline blessing.

dynamic_token_status.esy-variable-qanona: IMPLEMENTED — v2.8.1: updated to layered rendering for Sapra/Ramsha/Lelya
dynamic_token_status.esy-variable-barekmar-intercessions: IMPLEMENTED — v2.8.1: all 9 required components now present in east-syriac.json

barekmar_components.note: Added v2.8.1. All appended to end of components/east-syriac.json.
components: esy-barekmar-general — fallback; rendered when no day-specific or seasonal form found
esy-barekmar-sauma — seasonal Lenten form; rendered throughout Sauma season
esy-barekmar-sunday — Sunday diptychs; Resurrection and assembly character
esy-barekmar-monday — Monday diptychs; apostolic and missionary character
esy-barekmar-tuesday — Tuesday diptychs; teaching and prophetic character
esy-barekmar-wednesday — Wednesday diptychs; penitential character (fasting day)
esy-barekmar-thursday — Thursday diptychs; sacramental and priestly character
esy-barekmar-friday — Friday diptychs; martyr-honouring, penitential (fasting day)
esy-barekmar-saturday — Saturday diptychs; commemorative of the departed, anticipatory

legacy_sequences.note: ramsha-sequence, sapra-sequence, and lelya-sequence retained in rubrics.json for backward compatibility. renderEastSyriac() now routes exclusively to the cathedral-* and monastic-* named variants.

ethiopian_tradition.status: OPERATIONAL — v2.8.1. Full 24-hour canonical cycle, Tselote Meweta opening, Senkessar wired, Metsehafe Tselot rubric aesthetic. Mahlet text fully expanded.
ethiopian_tradition.entry_point: selectMode('ethiopian-saatat') — sets window._forcedOfficeId, hides BCP sidebar, shows ethiopian-settings, applies .ethiopian-theme to body
ethiopian_tradition.rubrics_load: Loaded in hydrateForEthiopianSaatat() and concatenated onto appData.rubrics
components: eth-introduction-to-every-hour — (v2.6.1) Tselote Meweta mandatory opening: prostration rubric, Lord's Prayer, Thanksgiving, Psalm 50/51 with Gloria Patri
eth-basalios-prayer — St. Basil's foundational prayer (all hours)
eth-prayer-thanksgiving — RETIRED FROM SEQUENCE v2.6.1. Content subsumed by eth-introduction-to-every-hour. Retained in JSON as reference.
eth-anqasa-birhan — Gate of Light Theotokos hymn; appended after Mazmur
eth-nigatu-hour-text — Matins (06:00–09:00)
eth-meserk-hour-text — Third Hour / Mese'rk (09:00–12:00)
eth-lika-hour-text — Sixth Hour / Lika (12:00–15:00). Crucifixion hour. ID is 'lika' not 'liku' — corrected in rubrics metadata v2.6.1.
eth-terk-hour-text — Ninth Hour / Tese'at (15:00–17:00)
eth-serkh-hour-text — Eleventh Hour / Vespers / Serkh (17:00–18:00)
eth-nome-hour-text — Compline / Nime (18:00–21:00)
eth-hour-7 — First Night Watch / Le'lit (21:00–00:00). Text complete. Phase 2B stale note removed v2.8.1.
eth-lelit-hour-text — Midnight / Le'lit (00:00–03:00)
eth-mahlet-hour-text — Pre-dawn Vigil / Mahlet (03:00–06:00). Text fully expanded v2.8.1 — Gethsemane imagery, holy women at the tomb, Marian intercession, eschatological frame.
eth-mazmur-tuat — Psalm metadata for Tuat
eth-leke-haile-chant — 12-fold Leke Haile doxology (Full edition only)
eth-41-kyrie — 41-fold Kyrie / Igzee'o Tesahalene (Full edition only)
eth-saints-commemoration — Senkessar pipeline component + Oriental saints fallback + intercession fallback
eth-synaxarium-reading — Senkessar reading scaffold; actual pipeline implemented within eth-saints-commemoration handler
eth-weddase-sunday through eth-weddase-saturday — Weddase Maryam for all seven days
sequence_current: eth-saatat-hour-slot            ← active canonical hour + Sixth Hour sentence
eth-mazmur-slot                 ← Psalms + Anqasa Birhan (+ Leke Haile in Full)

12:00\u201315:00.note: Crucifixion hour — commemorative sentence injected in renderer

ethiopian_tradition.saints_filter: Only saints where tradition includes 'ethiopian' or 'oriental' (case-insensitive). Used only when no Senkessar entry found. Final fallback: generic Oriental Orthodox Communion intercession.
ethiopian_tradition.bcp_isolation: All BCP-specific blocks gated behind !isEthiopianSaatat throughout renderOffice()

ethiopian_synaxarium.status: OPERATIONAL — Fully wired and rendering. All 13 months confirmed complete.
ethiopian_synaxarium.description: Senkessar (Ethiopian Synaxarium) integrated in eth-saints-commemoration rubric handler. Renders for all hours via EthiopianCalendar date resolution and dynamic fetch from data/synaxarium/ethiopian/{month}/{day}.json.
ethiopian_synaxarium.wiring_status: COMPLETE. EthiopianCalendar.getEthiopianDate() called directly in eth-saints-commemoration. Senkessar index lazy-loaded and cached. Per-day narratives fetched and cached.

miazia_path_resolution.issue: EthiopianCalendar outputs 'Miyazya'. Folder on disk is 'miazia'.
miazia_path_resolution.status: RESOLVED IN RENDERER — MONTH_SLUG_MAP maps 'miyazya': 'miazia'. No folder rename required.
miazia_path_resolution.optional_cleanup: Cosmetic only: consider aligning senkessar-index.json month 8 slug to 'miazia' in a future maintenance pass.

narrative_format.narrative_style: Deep Assembly — High-liturgical English; 4-paragraph woven structure
narrative_format.register: Formal high-liturgical English; no modern idioms; paragraph breaks as \n\n

vespers.json.status: BASELINE COMPLETE — fixed/invariable content complete. v1.2: daily-prokeimenon and vesperal-reading resolved. v1.3: Octoechos tone engine; Saturday stichera/aposticha fully populated (tones 1–8). v1.4: Monday–Friday stichera/aposticha materially populated (tones 1–8). v1.5: kathisma-reading resolved for ordinary Mon–Sat as appointment rubric. v1.6: troparion-or-apolytikion — Saturday full Resurrectional Troparion; Bright Week Paschal Troparion; Mon–Fri tone stub; Sunday deferred. v1.7: theotokion-dismissal — Saturday full dismissal Theotokion (tones 1–8, distinct from Dogmatikon); Bright Week explicit omission; Mon–Fri tone stub; Sunday deferred. v1.8: Sunday Small Vespers implemented as a real baseline path. All five Sunday-case variable slots resolved: stichera/aposticha use resurrectional tone corpus with explicit Sunday Small Vespers labeling; kathisma-reading resolves to actionable omission rubric; troparion and theotokion resolve to full tone texts (type:'text'). Every variable slot across all day-of-week paths now yields full text, an explicit rubric stub, or an explicit omission rubric. v1.9: weekday theotokion-dismissal (Mon–Fri) upgraded from tone-identified rubric stub to type:'text' full resolution using new data/horologion/vespers-weekday-theotokion.json (40 entries: 8 tones × 5 weekdays). Mon/Tue/Thu: standard Octoechos weekday Theotokion. Wed/Fri: Stavrotheotokion (Cross Theotokion). resolvedAs:'weekday-octoechos-theotokion'. Engine v3.5: feast-rank arbitration live — _resolveFeastOverrideContext() governs troparion/apolytikion and dismissal Theotokion; season-aware qualification (ranks 1–4 outside Great Lent weekdays; ranks 1–2 only during Great Lent weekdays). Engine v3.7: _resolveFestalRubricOverrideSlot() governs stichera-at-lord-i-have-cried and aposticha feast override. Remaining work: full festal text beyond rubric stubs; kathisma full psalm text; Triodion/Pentecostarion overlay.

schema.json.status: Active — documents normalized payload shape v1

octoechos-vespers.json.coverage: Tones 1–8. Saturday: full resurrectional stichera and aposticha (unchanged from v1.3). Monday–Friday: materially populated with baseline ordinary weekday stichera and aposticha (v1.4). Sunday: baseline_stub:true + sunday_deferred:true — explicitly deferred; renders as tone-identified rubric stub. Not silently empty.
octoechos-vespers.json.keying: Outer key: tone string '1'–'8'. Inner key: weekday name (saturday, sunday, monday … friday).
octoechos-vespers.json.content_source: English Octoechos (Antiochian/OCA Hapgood-tradition resurrectional texts). Saturday texts are representative first stichera; full 3–6 stichera set per slot is a future data-pass item.

weekday_themes.tuesday: St. John the Baptist and the Prophets

vespers-kathisma.json.coverage: 7 entries keyed by JS Date.getDay() index (0=Sunday through 6=Saturday). Mon–Sat: kathisma number, title, LXX psalm range, Protestant equivalents, incipit. Sunday: sunday_deferred:true with explicit note. Rule: standard Byzantine Psalter weekly cycle for ordinary Vespers (OCA/Antiochian Psalter table; Jordanville Horologion 2008). Overrides not implemented: great-feasts, great-lent, bright-week, sunday-vespers.

vespers-troparion.json.status: Active — Horologion v1.8 (2026-03-07) — unchanged in v1.9 and Menaion v1
vespers-troparion.json.coverage: resurrectional_troparia: 8 entries keyed by tone string ('1'–'8'). Each entry: tone number, title, full liturgical text (Antiochian/OCA Hapgood-tradition English). paschal_troparion: Paschal Troparion for Bright Week use. Applicable days: Saturday Great Vespers and Sunday Small Vespers (both resolved; same Octoechos apolytikion for both offices). Weekday troparion text (Mon–Fri): this file is NOT the source for weekday troparion. Weekday troparion is resolved via data/menaion/ + js/menaion-resolver.js (all 12 months active; feast-rank arbitration live via _resolveFeastOverrideContext() v3.5). Season-aware feast qualification rule: ranks 1–4 qualify outside Great Lent weekdays; ranks 1–2 only during Great Lent weekdays. Falls back to weekday-theme rubric when no qualifying Menaion troparion is resolved. Source: Byzantine Octoechos; Antiochian/OCA Hapgood-tradition English. Remaining overrides not yet implemented: full festal apolytikia text (currently rubric stubs), great-lent full text.

vespers-theotokion.json.status: Active — Horologion v1.8 (2026-03-07) — unchanged in v1.9
vespers-theotokion.json.coverage: dismissal_theotokia: 8 entries keyed by tone string ('1'–'8'). Each entry: tone number, title, full liturgical text (Antiochian/OCA Hapgood-tradition English). Applicable days: Saturday Great Vespers and Sunday Small Vespers (both resolved in v1.8; same Octoechos apolytikion-paired dismissal Theotokion). These are the dismissal Theotokia of the Octoechos, distinct from the Dogmatika (major Theotokia sung during the Aposticha). Weekday dismissal Theotokion is now handled by vespers-weekday-theotokion.json (v1.9). Feast-rank Theotokion arbitration is live via _resolveFeastOverrideContext() (v3.5) — season-aware qualification applies. Remaining not yet implemented: full festal Theotokion text (currently rubric stubs), great-lent Theotokion override.

small-compline.json.coverage: Skeleton for Small Compline (Mikron Apodeipnon). officeKey:'small-compline', tradition:'BYZC'. Fixed slots (usual-beginning, psalm-50, psalm-69, psalm-142, doxology, creed, trisagion-prayers, compline-theotokion, prayer-of-basil, into-thy-hands) are placeholders resolved at runtime by _resolveComplineSlots() from compline-fixed.json. Variable slot troparion-of-the-day resolved via Menaion resolver with full feast-rank arbitration: festal troparion override active (season-aware, ranks 1–2 qualify during Great Lent weekdays; ranks 1–4 outside); Great Lent seasonal troparion layer active (_resolveComplineSeasonalTroparionSlot(), v3.9); festal Theotokion arbitration active (_resolveComplineFestalTheotokionRubric(), v4.4); office-specific fallback normalization active. Verified: 2026-03-02 Small Compline → compline-lenten-rubric; 2026-03-25 Small Compline → menaion-feast-troparion.

compline-fixed.json.status: Active — Horologion v3.0 (2026-03-08) — NEW in v3.0
compline-fixed.json.coverage: Invariable fixed texts for Small Compline. All entries resolved unconditionally by _resolveComplineSlots(). Slots keyed by name: usual-beginning, psalm-50, psalm-69, psalm-142, doxology, creed, trisagion-prayers, compline-theotokion, prayer-of-basil, into-thy-hands. Psalm translations follow LXX numeration used in Byzantine practice. Source: Standard Antiochian/OCA Horologion (Jordanville 2008 edition).

first-hour.json.coverage: Skeleton for the First Hour (Prima). officeKey:'first-hour', tradition:'BYZC'. Fixed slots resolved from first-hour-fixed.json: usual-beginning, psalm-5, psalm-89, psalm-100 (LXX), trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-first-hour. Variable slot troparion-of-the-day: feast-rank arbitration active (season-aware; ranks 1–2 qualify during Great Lent weekdays; ranks 1–4 outside); Great Lent seasonal troparion layer active (v3.8); office-specific fallback normalization active (v4.0). Theotokion slot: normalization active (v4.1); festal Theotokion rubric arbitration active (v4.3). Verified: 2026-03-02 First Hour → little-hour-lenten-rubric; 2026-03-25 First Hour → menaion-feast-troparion.

first-hour-fixed.json.status: Active — Horologion v3.1 (2026-03-08) — NEW in v3.1
first-hour-fixed.json.coverage: Fixed-text corpus for the First Hour. Invariable slots: usual-beginning, psalm-5 (LXX), psalm-89 (LXX), psalm-100 (LXX), trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-first-hour. Variable slots (troparion-of-the-day, first-hour-theotokion) are not stored here; resolved dynamically by HorologionEngine. Source: Jordanville 2008 edition.

third-hour.json.coverage: Skeleton for the Third Hour (Tertia). officeKey:'third-hour', tradition:'BYZC'. Fixed slots resolved from third-hour-fixed.json: usual-beginning, psalm-16, psalm-24, psalm-50 (LXX), trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-third-hour. Variable troparion and Theotokion slots: same engine arbitration as First Hour — feast-rank override, Great Lent seasonal layer, Theotokion normalization and festal arbitration all live.

third-hour-fixed.json.status: Active — Horologion v3.2 (2026-03-08) — NEW in v3.2
third-hour-fixed.json.coverage: Fixed-text corpus for the Third Hour. Invariable slots: usual-beginning, psalm-16 (LXX), psalm-24 (LXX), psalm-50 (LXX), trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-third-hour. Variable slots resolved dynamically by HorologionEngine. Source: Jordanville 2008 edition.

sixth-hour.json.coverage: Skeleton for the Sixth Hour (Sexta). officeKey:'sixth-hour', tradition:'BYZC'. Fixed slots resolved from sixth-hour-fixed.json: usual-beginning, psalm-53, psalm-54, psalm-90 (LXX), trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-sixth-hour. Variable troparion and Theotokion slots: same engine arbitration as First Hour.

sixth-hour-fixed.json.status: Active — Horologion v3.3 (2026-03-08) — NEW in v3.3
sixth-hour-fixed.json.coverage: Fixed-text corpus for the Sixth Hour. Invariable slots: usual-beginning, psalm-53 (LXX), psalm-54 (LXX), psalm-90 (LXX), trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-sixth-hour. Variable slots resolved dynamically by HorologionEngine. Source: Jordanville 2008 edition.

ninth-hour.json.coverage: Skeleton for the Ninth Hour (Nona). officeKey:'ninth-hour', tradition:'BYZC'. Fixed slots resolved from ninth-hour-fixed.json: usual-beginning, psalm-83, psalm-84, psalm-85 (LXX), trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-ninth-hour. Variable troparion and Theotokion slots: same engine arbitration as First Hour.

ninth-hour-fixed.json.status: Active — Horologion v3.4 (2026-03-08) — NEW in v3.4
ninth-hour-fixed.json.coverage: Fixed-text corpus for the Ninth Hour. Invariable slots: usual-beginning, psalm-83 (LXX), psalm-84 (LXX), psalm-85 (LXX), trisagion-prayers, prayer-of-the-ninth-hour. Variable slots resolved dynamically by HorologionEngine. Source: Jordanville 2008 edition.

vespers-weekday-theotokion.json.status: Active — Horologion v1.9 (2026-03-07) — NEW in v1.9
vespers-weekday-theotokion.json.coverage: weekday_theotokia: 40 entries keyed by tone string ('1'–'8') then weekday name ('monday'|'tuesday'|'wednesday'|'thursday'|'friday'). Each entry: tone, weekday, title, full liturgical text (Antiochian/OCA Hapgood-tradition English). Monday/Tuesday/Thursday: standard Octoechos weekday Theotokion for that tone + day. Wednesday/Friday: Stavrotheotokion (Cross Theotokion) — standard Byzantine fasting-day Theotokia. Applicable days: Mon–Fri ordinary weekday Vespers. Resolves theotokion-dismissal slot for all five weekdays at baseline. Limitation: baseline uses current Octoechos weekly tone; if Menaion troparion is of a different tone, Theotokion should follow Menaion tone — not yet implemented. Overrides not implemented: great-feasts, great-lent, Menaion-tone conflict.

coe_calendar_model.description: RESOLVED — v2.8.9. Three-layer COE calendar model implemented. Layer 1: season engine (calendar-east-syriac.js getSeason() / getDayClass()) operational. Layer 2: getDayClass() returns commemorations[] for Sawma Rabba Fridays and pre-Sawma Faithful Departed. Layer 3: CoeEligibility.filter() allowlist gate implemented; display re-enabled in renderEastSyriac(). COE-IIB audit complete: 133 identity IDs (136 rows) removed from commemorations.json. saint-grid model eliminated. No saint-grid fallback. Layer 2 renders before Layer 3 lookup. Silence is correct when no eligible saint is present.
coe_calendar_model.architectural_principle: Latin logic: Calendar = Saints framework with seasonal overlay. East Syriac logic: Calendar = Salvation-historical seasons with occasional saints.

layer_2_fixed_feasts.description: Major fixed observances calculated seasonally, not stored as date-keyed saints.

layer_3_individual_saints.status: COMPLETE — COE-IIB audit done; CoeEligibility.filter() gate active; display re-enabled v2.8.7
layer_3_individual_saints.description: Sparse. Conservative. Person-based only. Pre-schism and explicitly East Syriac.
exclusion_criteria: Eastern Orthodox saints not in COE usage
Saints in Roman Martyrology but not in Hudra
Saints included only because they are 'early enough'
Gregory the Great — disputed, currently tagged COE, needs review
Any saint tagged COE solely by proximity to EOR
layer_3_individual_saints.action: No further internal action required. One external-research holdout remains: mar-augustine / mar-augustine-commemoration — excluded from Layer 3 by the eligibility gate. Do not alter on internal evidence alone. Resolution requires external Syriac manuscript evidence (Budge Book of the Governors, Wright Catalogue, Encyclopaedia Iranica COE calendar). If a matching Hudra figure is confirmed: correct feast dates, merge identity records, add to allowlist. If no figure is found: REMOVE_COE_TAG from both records.

friday_logic.description: Friday is the martyrial axis of the East Syriac week throughout most seasons.

great_fast_fridays.name: Commemoration of the Holy Martyrs
great_fast_fridays.note: Corporate, not individual. All Fridays of Sawma Rabba. No rotating named saint.

friday_logic.implementation: getSeason() already returns season. Add getDayClass(date) to calendar-east-syriac.js returning { isFriday, isLenten, commemorationType, commemorationName }. renderEastSyriac() consults this before any saint lookup.

season_weekly_logic.subara: Text-heavy, saint-light. Marian material doxological. No Immaculate Conception construct.
season_weekly_logic.nativity: Synaxis-type commemorations. Apostles and biblical figures in propers. Major feasts are feast/commemoration type.
season_weekly_logic.denha: Occasional fixed apostles. Strong baptismal theology. Friday commemorations continue.
season_weekly_logic.sawma_rabba: Friday = Commemoration of the Martyrs (corporate). No rotating individual saint list. Inject dynamically.
season_weekly_logic.qyamta: Apostolic figures more visible. Thomas receives liturgical prominence. Paschal weeks suppress martyr themes.
season_weekly_logic.shlihe: Apostles commemorated strongly. Missionary saints possible (pre-schism). Individual apostle entries appropriate.
season_weekly_logic.eliya_sliwa: Prophetic focus. Prophets and OT figures appropriate.

ui_framing.avoid: 'Saint of the Day' framing for COE
ui_framing.recommended_addition: Display week name/number prominently within season for COE.

coe_calendar_model.action: No internal action required. COE-II complete. Optional future work: COE-IIC — expand Layer 2 with major Hudra fixed feasts (Denha/Epiphany, Sleeba/Exaltation of the Cross, Annunciation, All Saints). External holdout: see three_layer_model.layer_3_individual_saints.action.
coe_calendar_model.research_credit: Architectural model established 2026-02-28 via external liturgical consultant review.
